diff:
diff --git a/src/preferences.py b/src/preferences.py
index deba08a..06edf33 100644
--- a/src/preferences.py
+++ b/src/preferences.py
@@ -1,9 +1,29 @@
 def merge_preferences(defaults: dict, overrides: dict) -> dict:
+    """Merge two preference dictionaries safely.
+
+    Performs a shallow copy of :pycode:`defaults` and deep copies any mutable
+    nested structures so the original inputs are never mutated. The return value
+    contains the merged preferences.
+    """
+    # Start with a shallow copy of defaults â€“ nested structures must be
+    # duplicated explicitly.
     merged = defaults.copy()
-    notifications = merged.setdefault("notifications", {})
-    notifications.update(overrides.get("notifications", {}))
-    tags = merged.setdefault("tags", [])
-    tags.extend(overrides.get("tags", []))
+
+    # Handle notifications dict â€“ create a copy of the default if present
+    default_notifs = defaults.get("notifications", {})
+    merged_notifs = default_notifs.copy()
+    # Merge overrides into the copied notifications dict
+    merged_notifs.update(overrides.get("notifications", {}))
+    merged["notifications"] = merged_notifs
+
+    # Handle tags list â€“ create a copy of the default if present
+    default_tags = defaults.get("tags", [])
+    merged_tags = list(default_tags)
+    # Extend with overrides tags
+    merged_tags.extend(overrides.get("tags", []))
+    merged["tags"] = merged_tags
+
+    # Copy any other keys from overrides
     for key, value in overrides.items():
         if key not in {"notifications", "tags"}:
             merged[key] = value
